The final June services and composite reports were revised higher from the preliminary estimates. The services PMI is at 52.3, up from the initial estimate of 51.3 (50.9 in May). The composite improved to 52.0 from the 50.7 flash estimate and 50.3 in May. Still, the average in Q2 was 50.3 compared with 50.9 in Q1 25 and Q4 24. It is the lowest quarterly average since Q3 23. The UK economy appears to have gone from the top performer in the G7 in Q1 to one of the slowest in Q2, which exacerbates the fiscal challenge.

CAD: The Canadian dollar was the strongest G10 currency yesterday, gaining about 0.40% against the greenback. The Canadian dollar's gains seemed to reflect a little catch-up in a firmer greenback environment. While several other major currencies made new highs for the year this week, the Canadian dollar was not one of them. The US dollar's low for the year was recorded on June 16 near CAD1.3540. Yesterday's low matched Tuesday's low near CAD1.3590. It edged down to about CAD1.3580 today. The June manufacturing PMI fell to 45.6 from 46.1 in May, which was the first gain of the year (45.3 in April, the cyclical low. The US tariffs, real and anticipated, are taking a toll. The Bank of Canada has front-loaded its rate cuts, but the Bank of Canada turned somewhat cautious as the perceived neutral rate is approached. While the market is warming up to a rate cut soon, the next cut is not fully discounted until Q4. Canada reports May merchandise trade balance today. There has been dramatic deterioration this year. Through April, the goods deficit was about C$8.3 bln. In the first four months of 2024, the trade deficit was almost C$3.6 bln. Canada's merchandise exports slid 10.8% in April, the largest drop since the end of 2008 and reached their lowest level since June 2023, as shipments to the US tumbled by 15.7%. Exports of light vehicles and parts fell by nearly a quarter.

AUD: The Australian dollar was sold to almost $0.6540 as the greenback recovered broadly, but buyers re-emerged on the dip and lifted the Aussie to new session highs to almost $0.6590. It stalled near the upper Bollinger Band, which it has frayed in recent sessions (found ~$0.6600 today). It is trading with a softer bias today (~$0.6565-$0.6590). The final June services and composite PMI were also revised higher from the preliminary estimates. The composite was revised to 51.6 from 51.2 initially and 50.5 in May. It averaged 51.0 in Q2 and 51.1 in Q1. Separately, Australia's merchandise trade balance narrowed for the second consecutive month in May. The A$2.2 bln surplus was half of what was expected and compares with A$5.6 bln surplus in May 2024 and $A5.4 bln in April 2025. Exports fell (2.7% month-over-month), while imports jumped (3.8%). In the first five months of the year, the trade surplus averaged about A$4.1 bln compared with an average of A$5.6 bln in Jan-May 2024. The futures market lifted the chances of a rate cut next week to more than 100% from about 65% on Tuesday. Tomorrow, Australia reports May household spending. The softer than expected retail sales (0.2% vs. 0.5% median forecast in Bloomberg's survey) warn of downside risks, the median forecast for a 0.5% increase in household spending.

MXN: The dollar posted an inside day against the Mexican peso yesterday. Still the greenback eked out a minor gain (<0.0.3%) and settled higher for the first time in eight sessions. It has held below MXN18.82 today and is near MXN18.77 in European turnover. Tuesday's low was slightly above MXN18.66. The week's high was set on Monday, slightly below MXN18.90. Yesterday, Mexico reported domestic auto sales slowed by 3.25% in June after the nearly 10.8% surge in May. It is a volatile series, but the sales in H1 25 (~118.2k monthly average) were slightly higher than in H1 24 (~117.7k monthly average). Today, Mexico reports April fixed investment and private consumption. It rose on a year-over-year basis in March (1.16%) after contracting for the previous three months. The base effect makes for a difficult comparison this year, and the median forecast is for a 3.6% year-over-year decline, which would be the largest since early 2021. The swaps market expected Banxico to pause its easing course, with the overnight cash rate target at 8%. The terminal rate is seen between 7.25% and 7.50% a year from now.

Original Post

Editor's Note: The summary bullets for this article were chosen by Seeking Alpha editors.

data.title: 3 Of The Best Income Machines To Retire On Today
data.description:
When looking to retire on dividends, you want to invest in exceptional income machines. The makings of an excellent income machine are ones that pay out attractive current yields but also can be counted on to continue raising those payouts over the long term at a rate that meets or beats inflation. This requires that they be run by good management teams, have durable business models, have strong balance sheets, and have a proven track record of being able to grow their dividends through thick and thin.

With this in mind, in this article, I am going to share three exceptional income machines that are among the very best for those looking to retire on dividends.

Income Machine #1

The first income machine I want to talk about is the Schwab U.S. Dividend Equity ETF (SCHD). The reason I like it so much is that it has arguably the best combination of current yield and long-term dividend growth of any ETF that I have seen. Between its near 4% dividend yield—which positions retirees to follow the 4% rule and live off of dividends alone—as well as a 13-year consecutive dividend growth streak and a 10-year dividend CAGR of 10.77%, SCHD gives investors the certainty that their dividends are going to grow year after year at a pace that easily beats inflation while also delivering enough current yield to enable them to live off of dividends today, assuming they have been saving with the 4% rule in mind.

This makes it arguably a much more sound approach to investing for retirement than investing in a popular index fund like the SPDR S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and simply liquidating shares over time because the dividend payments not only come in each year, but they have risen each year for 13 years and at a pace that has crushed inflation for that period of time. This mitigates the sequence-of-returns risk with SCHD's dividend investing approach, rather than hoping that SPY can deliver good years and avoid any prolonged slumps.

Adding further confidence to SCHD as an income machine for retirement is the fact that it has 103 holdings, all of which are blue-chip dividend growth stocks diversified across the energy, consumer defensive, healthcare, technology, industrials, consumer cyclical, financials, and communications sectors, with a small allocation to basic materials and utilities. Its largest holdings are the likes of Texas Instruments (TXN), Cisco Systems (CSCO), ConocoPhillips (COP), Chevron Corporation (CVX), Merck & Co. (MRK), PepsiCo (PEP), Verizon Communications (VZ), Coca-Cola Co. (KO), AbbVie (ABBV), and Altria (MO), all of which are proven and competitively positioned businesses that pay out attractive dividends and grow their dividends consistently over time while backing them with strong balance sheets.

To top it all off, SCHD charges a mere 0.06% expense ratio, making it a very cost-efficient fund to invest in, thereby saving shareholders considerable money over the long term that would have otherwise been lost to fees.

Income Machine #2

Another great income machine to retire on today is Enbridge (ENB). ENB is a midstream infrastructure company (MLPX) that issues a 1099 tax form, though it is a Canadian corporation, so keep potential tax implications in mind as well as the fact that if you hold it in a U.S. retirement account, you can be exempted from the withholding tax due to a tax treaty between Canada and the United States.

It is such a good pick for retirees because it generates very low-risk cash flows, with over 98% of its EBITDA either regulated or take-or-pay contracted, while over 95% of its counterparties are investment-grade rated. Additionally, about 80% of its EBITDA is inflation-protected, while less than 1% of its EBITDA is tied to commodity pricing. It generates its EBITDA from over 200 asset streams and businesses, making it very well diversified as it has liquids pipelines, gas transmission, regulated natural gas utilities, and renewable power assets that make up its well-diversified business model.

On top of that, it has a strong balance sheet as evidenced by its BBB+ credit rating, and it maintains a prudent amount of leverage in the 4.5 to 5.0 times debt-to-EBITDA range, which is reasonable given its highly regulated cash flow stream. The company also generates a lot of cash flow, with between $4 and $5 billion in free cash flow generated before capex each year, and it targets a distributable cash flow payout ratio between 60% and 70%, which gives it plenty of retained cash flow to invest in growth opportunities.

Additionally, it has grown its dividend for 30 straight years, making it a Dividend Aristocrat (NOBL) and a very dependable income investment. In fact, management expects 5% annualized EBITDA growth through the end of the decade, which analysts concur with as they have a 5.1% EBITDA CAGR forecasted through the end of 2029.

This is very believable given its large capital program across all its businesses, in particular its natural gas transmission and natural gas distribution and storage businesses.

Last but not least, it has a current dividend yield of 6.2% and an expected EBITDA growth rate that should exceed inflation, as well as a strong balance sheet, business model, and track record. Therefore, ENB makes for a very attractive income machine to retire on today.

Income Machine #3

The final income machine I am going to discuss today is Main Street Capital Corporation (MAIN), which is a business development company (BIZD). It has arguably the best long-term track record of any publicly traded BDC out there, thanks to its highly skilled internal management team that gives it a very low cost of management relative to its externally managed peers like Ares Capital Corporation (ARCC) and even Blackstone Secured Lending (BXSL), while also allocating capital very effectively. This, in turn, has earned it a huge premium to net asset value in its valuation, which currently sits at 88%, enabling it to issue shares very accretively, thereby growing book value and net investment income per share. This also fuels its ability to grow its dividend over time, even in environments where the underlying business may be facing some interest headwinds.

Its base dividend has grown steadily over time, while it has also paid out substantial supplemental dividends over the years. This can be seen in the graph below, where supplemental dividends have created some volatility year-to-year, but the long-term upward trajectory is impressive. It has a 6.5% dividend per share CAGR since 2008, which is about two to three times the long-term average rate of inflation. In addition, the net asset value per share has risen at a 5.9% CAGR over that period, further reflecting the company's ability to compound wealth for shareholders, even as it pays out a large amount in dividends each year.

With a next-12-month dividend yield of 6.9% and MAIN’s impressive track record of delivering market-crushing total returns over the long term—backed by a portfolio that is invested in a mix of first-lien senior-secured debt investments, as well as other middle-market equity and debt investments that earn high returns on equity, and an investment-grade balance sheet that is conservatively financed with just a 0.6 times debt-to-NAV ratio that is well below most of its peers—MAIN looks like a wonderful retirement income machine right now.

Investor Takeaway

SCHD, ENB, and MAIN are phenomenal income machines that pay out attractive current dividends in combination with being highly dependable inflation-beating dividend growers and defensive in their underlying business models and balance sheets. This makes them very suitable for retirees looking to fund their retirement not only with sufficient current income, but income that will outpace inflation over the long term. These are the sorts of opportunities we target in our retirement portfolio at High Yield Investor.

data.title: Ignite Your Big Beautiful Portfolio: Best Stocks To Buy Before 4th Of July
data.description:
Senate Passes Big Beautiful Bill: What’s the House Vote?

Following days of amendments, votes, and holdouts, President Trump’s tax bill, formerly titled the “One Big Beautiful Bill Act,” was approved by the Senate on Tuesday by a razor-thin 51-50 margin. Vice President JD Vance cast the tie-breaking vote, representing a major political victory for the administration. But what’s in the megabill that is now with the House, which requires another vote?

What is Trump’s tax cut bill?

The ‘Big Beautiful Bill’ is focused on a wide range of policy changes and tax cuts. While still requiring a vote from the House, the bill delivers on several of Trump’s campaign pledges, including:

  • Extension of 2017 Tax Cuts: The bill extended some tax breaks that were set to expire under Trump’s 2017 Tax Cuts and Jobs Act.

  • New Tax Breaks: The bill extends new exemptions for overtime pay, car loan interest payments, and no tax on tips. It also offers tax breaks for seniors making less than $75,000. Seniors can receive a $6,000 deduction.

  • Child Tax Credit: The bill proposes increasing the child tax credit.

  • SALT Deduction Cap: It raises the cap on the state and local tax (SALT) deduction, with the Senate proposing a temporary increase to $40,000 from $10,000.

  • Business Tax Cuts: The bill includes business provisions for the immediate write-off of 100% of the cost of equipment and research.

The bill also allocates $350B toward national security initiatives and border enforcement like:

  • Defense Spending: Additional defense funding, including the development of a "Golden Dome" missile defense shield.

  • Border Wall Funding: Funding for continued construction of a border wall with Mexico.

  • Expansion and Increased Staffing: More funding for Immigration and Customs Enforcement officers and Border Patrol agents, and expansion of detention facilities.

  • Immigration Fees: Proposes increases and new fees for immigration services and asylum applications.

Estimated to cost $3.3T (a combination of nearly $4.5T in tax cuts with $1.2T in spending reductions), the overall bill offers a package that Republicans argue will stimulate economic growth, while Democrats warn it will increase job losses and exacerbate fiscal challenges including:

  • The US Deficit: The Congressional Budget Office (CBO) estimates the bill will increase the US national debt by $3.9T.

  • Disproportionately Impacts Low-Income Earners: Democrats and Republicans have expressed concerns that cut to Medicaid and SNAP would disproportionately harm low-income individuals and families.

  • Medicaid Cuts: Reductions in Medicaid funding raise concerns about millions of people losing health insurance coverage.

  • SNAP (formerly known as Food Stamps) Restrictions: In 2024, over 40M people relied on some form of Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program. The proposed cuts, which include increased work requirements, could shift a percentage of costs to states.

  • Education Reforms: “Patriotic Education” would include cuts to federal education funding and involve potential reductions in student loan forgiveness.

  • Energy: Reductions to many clean-energy tax credits from the Inflation Reduction Act.

The next step in the legislative process involves a House vote, yet markets were mixed through midday trading Wednesday as investors digested the news.

Adding to potential defense spending, NATO pledged 5% of gross domestic product by 2035 at last week’s NATO summit, which could unlock $2.7T for US contractors. Some of the greatest value and growth can be found in diversifying portfolios with fundamentally strong companies. Discover high-conviction stock ideas that leverage advanced analytical tools to construct portfolios designed with the potential to outperform.

Amid the global tumult and Big Beautiful Bill, there has been significant investment in the defensive industrials, which is why I have screened for stocks that are:

  • Top Industrials

  • Focused on defense-oriented industries like:

    • Aerospace and Defense

    • Industrial Machinery & Supplies & Components

    • Construction Machinery & Heavy Transportation Equipment

    • Construction and Engineering

  • Quant-Rated Strong Buy or Buy

  • Market cap of $530M or greater

As investors look for stability among persistently high inflation, a slowdown in corporate earnings, the weaponization of tariffs, and geopolitical uncertainty, we’re bullish on six top industrial defense stocks.

Made in America: Top Industrial Defense Stocks

Trump’s “Made in America” push is one of the cornerstones of his economic philosophy of strengthening domestic industry, US job creation, and an effort to make American companies more globally competitive with less reliance on foreign nations. Although the industrial sector is considered cyclical, tending to be more susceptible to the influence of economic cycles, geopolitical and economic uncertainty, some industrial stocks can be more resilient amid uncertainty.

Increases in global defense spending have increased the demand for industrial defense companies that may capitalize on Trump’s Buy American agenda and the ‘Big Beautiful Bill.’ The megabill includes significant investment for defense products and services that could offer favorable opportunities and upside for companies that provide supply components for the defense sector. Let’s dive into my top six selections.

  1. Graham Corporation (GHM)

    • Market Capitalization: $536.47M

    • Quant Rating: Buy

    • Quant Sector Ranking (as of 7/2/25): 92 out of 614

    • Quant Industry Ranking (as of 7/2/25): 11 out of 74

    • Industry: Industrial Machinery & Supplies & Components

I first wrote about Graham Corp, Best Defense Stock, in March 2024. Since then, the stock has surged 86%. Despite the impressive gain, the valuation framework is nearly unchanged, while the company has continued to deliver exceptional growth, with a forward EPS growth rate of 61%.

Geopolitical tensions have heightened the demand for aerospace and defense technologies, and record world military expenditures in 2024 resulted in a 9.4% increase, its steepest year-over-year rise since. Military budgets in 2025 have risen, with the US military budget exceeding $962B – one of the largest in the world, nearly four times China’s $246B.

Graham Corporation is a diversified industrial machinery and supply component conglomerate capitalizing on the trend. Targeting $225M-$235M in fiscal 2026 revenue following its continued trend of topping earnings estimates, Graham Corporation CEO Daniel J. Thoren handed the company reigns to COO Matt Malone in June, following incredible Q4 2025 results that included 13% revenue growth, an adjusted EBITDA increase of 69% to $22.4 million or 10.7% as a percentage of sales and record backlog of $412 million as of March 31.

GHM’s year-over-year EPS diluted growth of 164% has outpaced the sector’s 5%, capitalizing on long-term multi-year contracts including a recent $136.5 million award from the US Navy for mission-critical equipment for the Virginia-class submarine program. GHM offers a strong balance sheet and overall financials, and although the company’s valuation is a bit stretched, its trailing PEG ratio of 0.27x versus the sector median of 1.23x is more than a -78% difference to the sector.

2. Mueller Industries, Inc. (MLI)

  • Market Capitalization: $9.08B

  • Quant Rating: Strong Buy

  • Quant Sector Ranking (as of 7/2/25): 50 out of 614

  • Quant Industry Ranking (as of 7/2/25): 4 out of 74

  • Industry: Industrial Machinery & Supplies & Components

Mueller Industries is a vertically integrated manufacturer and seller of a broad range of copper, brass, and aluminum components worldwide. Its broad range of diversified products are used in many industries, including military and defense, heating, ventilation, transportation, and more.

Rallying over 45% in the last year, the company has maintained a solid financial position. As the only vertically integrated manufacturer of copper tubes and fitting in North America, MLI drives shareholder value through its impressive 19% YoY revenue growth versus the sector’s 3%.

Additionally, MLK offers robust profitability with a 16% net income margin TTM, a 159% difference to the sector’s 6%. Its trailing Return on Total Capital and Return on Total Assets trumps the sectors, and the company offers a strong cash position.

Mueller also offers an attractive valuation in line with the sector. Its forward P/E Non-GAAP of 13.68x is nearly a 32% difference to the sector’s 20x, making it a solid consideration for a portfolio.

My next defensive stock is a leader in its industry and a stock that’s returned over 68% in one month for the PRO Quant Portfolio.

3. Power Solutions International, Inc. (PSIX)

  • Market Capitalization: $1.51B

  • Quant Rating: Strong Buy

  • Quant Sector Ranking (as of 7/2/25): 1 out of 61

  • Quant Industry Ranking (as of 7/2/25): 1 out of 12

  • Industry: Heavy Electrical Equipment

Featured as one of my Top 10 Stocks for H2 2025 and a current security in the PRO Quant Portfolio, small-cap stock, Power Solutions International has rallied an incredible +915% over the last year and +140% YTD.

Officially added to the Russell 2000 and MSCI US Small Cap indexes on June 30, 2025, PSIX has capitalized on the AI boom and demand for data centers. Posting a strong outlook and Q1 2025 sales of $135.4M, up 42% YoY and EPS of $0.83 that beat by $0.37, the designer, engineer, manufacturer, and seller of heavy electrical equipment has catapulted PSIX to success.

“This milestone highlights PSI’s growing presence in the power systems, data center products, and industrial engine markets, and we believe it will enhance our visibility with a broader base of institutional and retail investors as we continue executing on our strategic initiatives,” said Dino Xykis, chief executive officer of Power Solutions International.

Debt reduction and improving margins have aided its growth. Its fuel-agnostic strategy and expertise in off-road applications provide a unique competitive advantage. Despite its tremendous momentum, PSIX still trades at a discount compared to its peers, as showcased by a trailing PEG ratio of 0.11x versus the sector’s 1.23x. Next up is a company offering safety and specialty service solutions globally.

4. APi Group Corporation (APG)

  • Market Capitalization: $13.74B

  • Quant Rating: Strong Buy

  • Quant Sector Ranking (as of 7/2/25): 23 out of 614

  • Quant Industry Ranking (as of 7/2/25): 7 out of 36

  • Industry: Construction and Engineering

Offering end-to-end integrated services including fire protection, HVAC, construction, and engineering for various commercial, private, public, and governmental agencies, APi Group Corporation has demonstrated accelerated growth year-to-date, up nearly 40%.

Benefitting from an inspection-first strategy, the company’s strategic acquisition initiatives that included Chubb fire and security business from Carrier Global Corporation (CARR) in 2022 for an enterprise value of $3.1B have contributed to the company’s solid growth. APG offers 11.42% EBITDA Growth YoY compared to the sector median 6.82%, YoY ROE growth of 39% versus -5.13%, and strong cash flow. APi Group’s success has given way to its addition in the S&P MidCap 400 Index, replacing United States Steel (X). While the company trades in line with the sector as showcased by its C- Valuation Grade, its expansion in North American elevator services and progress towards strategic targets are expected to achieve 13% or more in adjusted EBITDA margin in 2025. Some additional financial targets include:

  • +$10B in net revenues by 2028

  • +16%+ adjusted EBITDA margin by 2028

  • +60% of net revenues from inspection, service, and monitoring divisions

  • +$3B in cumulative adjusted free cash flow through 2028

Russ Becker, APi’s President and Chief Executive Officer stated: “We are excited to share our new 10/16/60+ shareholder value creation framework and detail our next phase of profitable growth…We are off to a strong start in 2025 with positive momentum across the business, allowing us to reaffirm our second quarter and full year 2025 financial guidance. We are confident in our leaders’ ability to execute our strategy and deliver against our new 10/16/60+ long-term financial targets, creating value for all of our stakeholders.”

5. Douglas Dynamics, Inc. (PLOW)

  • Market Capitalization: $696.51M

  • Quant Rating: Strong Buy

  • Quant Sector Ranking (as of 7/2/25): 29 out of 614
